name: Trigonometric Landscape: The Sine-Cosine Surface
description: This interactive 3D surface plot visualizes the mathematical function z = sin(x) * cos(y). It generates a dynamic landscape of peaks and valleys, demonstrating how the product of two simple trigonometric functions can create a complex, wave-like surface in three dimensions. The interaction allows you to rotate and zoom, exploring the periodic nature and the intricate patterns formed by the sine and cosine waves interfering with each other across the XY-plane. This type of visualization is fundamental in understanding concepts like wave propagation, interference patterns, and scalar fields in physics and engineering.
